chapter: Move to the Modern Healthy Longevity Diet

ingredients.name: Dark green leafy vegetables

claims.claim: Rich in chlorophyll, folic acid, carotenoids and vitamin K, essential to neutralise excess acidity caused by Western diet
claims.mechanism: Folic acid supplementation caused a three-fold reduction in recurrence of precancerous colon lesions (adenomas) in a small RCT

claims.claim: Contain lutein and zeaxanthin important for protecting eye tissues and reducing cancer/cataract risk
claims.mechanism: Carotenoids lutein and zeaxanthin protect retinal tissue from oxidative damage

consumption.preparation: Raw or lightly steamed. Avoid overcooking by boiling which strips vitamins and minerals.
consumption.reasoning: Lowest calorie density and glycaemic load with highest concentration of vitamins, minerals and oligo-elements

relationships.description: Fat aids absorption of fat-soluble vitamins and carotenoids

ingredients.notable_nutrients: 100g cooked silver beet: 312% RDA vitamin K, 38% RDA vitamin A, 22% RDA vitamin C

claims.claim: Linked with reduced risk of several cancers including prostate, bladder and lung
claims.mechanism: Contain isothiocyanates, indol-3-carbinol and sulforaphane which trigger detoxifying enzymes

claims.claim: Sulforaphane triggers detoxifying enzymes protecting against colon and skin cancer, and in prevention of type 2 diabetes and autism
claims.mechanism: Sulforaphane activates phase 2 detoxification enzymes via Nrf2 pathway

claims.claim: Indole-3-carbinol is a powerful inhibitor of cancer-promoting enzyme WWP1, freeing tumour suppressor PTEN
claims.mechanism: Indole-3-carbinol inhibits WWP1 which normally blocks PTEN. PTEN prevents tumour growth. Mice with surplus PTEN live longer.
claims.study_ref: Published in Science journal. Refs 51, 52 in book

consumption.frequency: daily or several times per week
consumption.preparation: Raw or steamed 2-3 minutes only. Heat destroys active compounds rapidly. Combine with mustard (contains myrosinase enzyme) to convert glucoraphanin into isothiocyanate.
consumption.reasoning: Myrosinase in mustard activates the cancer-suppressor pathway

relationships.description: Mustard contains myrosinase enzyme which converts glucoraphanin into the cancer-suppressor isothiocyanate

claims.claim: One of the richest sources of the flavonoid quercetin with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and anticancer properties
claims.mechanism: Quercetin has shown to lower blood pressure and other metabolic syndrome factors in animal models. Quercetin and fisetin may kill abnormal senescent cells.

consumption.preparation: Raw, added to salads and dishes
consumption.reasoning: Extremely high polyphenol content: 654mg polyphenols and 3600mg antioxidants per 100g

relationships.description: Combining quercetin-rich foods (capers, apples) with genistein-rich soybeans blocks prostate and ovarian cancer cell growth at lower doses than either alone

claims.claim: Loaded with carotenoids essential for vitamin A production, eye health, immune function and prenatal development
claims.mechanism: Beta-carotene is converted to vitamin A which plays key role in eye and immune function

claims.claim: Contains polyacetylene with preventative effects against thrombosis and cancer
claims.mechanism: Polyacetylene compound found in Apiaceae family (carrots, celery, parsley) displays anti-thrombotic and anti-cancer activity

consumption.preparation: Steamed and pureed for 3x higher beta-carotene absorption than raw. Eat with fat (olive oil) for carotenoid absorption.
consumption.reasoning: Steaming and pureeing breaks cell walls, fat enables absorption of fat-soluble carotenoids

relationships.description: Fat needed at same meal to optimize carotenoid absorption and conversion to vitamin A

claims.claim: Contain the highest concentration of beta-carotene among common vegetables
claims.mechanism: 31mg beta-carotene per 100g cooked, converted to vitamin A for eye health and immune function

consumption.preparation: Cooked, paired with fat for absorption
consumption.reasoning: Highest beta-carotene concentration of common vegetables

claims.claim: Superior source of antioxidants due to generous amounts of alpha-carotene, zeaxanthin and lutein
claims.mechanism: Multiple carotenoid types provide broader antioxidant coverage than beta-carotene alone

consumption.preparation: Can eat thin slices raw, roast, bake, use in soups, or puree with chickpeas for hummus. Add to morning smoothies.
consumption.reasoning: Versatile, sweet flavour makes it easy to incorporate

claims.claim: Contain lycopene important in prevention of prostate, digestive tract and cervical cancers
claims.mechanism: Lycopene reduces IGF-I bioavailability, which may mediate anti-cancer effect. RCT showed reduction in prostate cancer growth with lycopene supplementation before surgery.

consumption.frequency: daily or several times per week
consumption.preparation: Cooked tomato puree results in greater lycopene elevation than raw tomato juice
consumption.reasoning: Cooking breaks cell walls and releases lycopene for better absorption

relationships.description: Combining garlic (S-allylcysteine) with tomatoes (lycopene) inhibits stomach cancer progression at much lower intakes than individually

claims.claim: Regular garlic-powder intake has protective effect against hypertension and age-dependent arterial stiffening
claims.mechanism: When crushed, alliin is exposed to alliinase producing allicin, which lowers blood pressure and cholesterol. Diallyl sulphide inhibits bowel cancer incidence by 74% in mice.

claims.claim: Associated with reduced risk of gastric, colon, prostate and endometrial cancers
claims.mechanism: Multiple bioactive organosulphur compounds including allicin and diallyl sulphide

consumption.preparation: Cut or crush to activate allicin. Heating destroys active allyl sulphur compound formation, so add raw garlic when possible or crush and let sit before cooking.
consumption.reasoning: Mechanical disruption (cutting/crushing) is required to produce the active compound allicin

relationships.description: Garlic (S-allylcysteine) + tomatoes (lycopene) inhibit stomach cancer at lower doses than either alone

claims.claim: One of the richest sources of dietary flavonoids important for cardiovascular disease and cancer prevention, especially gastric cancer
claims.mechanism: Contains fructooligosaccharides and thiosulfinates. Flavonoids prevent cardiovascular disease and cancer.

claims.claim: Italian sweet red onion Tropea contains taxifolin, a powerful inhibitor of many cancer cell types
claims.mechanism: Taxifolin directly inhibits cultured cancer cell growth. Red onions have highest flavonol levels; yellow onions contain half as much.

consumption.preparation: Red onions preferred for highest flavonol content. Raw or cooked.
consumption.reasoning: Red varieties contain double the flavonols of yellow onions

claims.claim: Curcumin has anti-inflammatory, antioxidant and anti-cancer effects, particularly relevant to colon cancer prevention
claims.mechanism: Curcumin affects gene activity and induces apoptosis (programmed death) of cancer cells. Inhibitory effects against colon, skin and oral cancer in rodents.

claims.claim: Six months of curcumin + quercetin treatment resulted in significant reduction of colon polyp number and size
claims.mechanism: Synergistic effect of curcumin and quercetin on colon polyp regression

claims.claim: May play a role in dementia prevention by inhibiting amyloid plaque formation
claims.mechanism: Curcumin inhibits formation of amyloid plaques associated with Alzheimer's disease

consumption.preparation: Fresh grated or dried powder. Combine with black pepper (piperine doubles curcumin blood levels) for absorption.
consumption.reasoning: Curcumin poorly absorbed alone (ref 87). Black pepper's piperine doubles blood levels (ref 88).

relationships.description: Piperine in black pepper doubles blood curcumin levels by improving absorption
Quercetin (in capers) + curcumin resulted in significant colon polyp reduction in pilot study

claims.claim: Contains piperine which doubles blood curcumin levels when combined with turmeric
claims.mechanism: Piperine inhibits metabolic clearance of curcumin, improving bioavailability

consumption.preparation: Freshly ground for maximum piperine content
consumption.reasoning: Primary value is as a bioavailability enhancer for curcumin

relationships.description: Piperine doubles blood curcumin levels

claims.claim: May play a role in prevention of obesity by activating TRPV1 and AMPK pathways
claims.mechanism: Capsaicin and capsaicinoids activate TRPV1 and AMPK molecular pathways. Inhibits inflammation in fat and liver cells, improves glucose control in animal studies.

claims.claim: Capsinoid supplementation associated with abdominal fat loss and increased energy expenditure
claims.mechanism: Capsinoids enhance heat-producing activity of brown adipose tissue, increasing energy expenditure over 6 weeks

consumption.preparation: Fresh or dried, added to dishes. Sweet peppers contain similar but less pungent compounds (capsinoids).
consumption.reasoning: Analgesic and metabolic benefits from capsaicin

claims.claim: Has potential anti-diabetic effects: may lower blood glucose and reverse fructose-induced insulin resistance
claims.mechanism: Hydroxycinnamaldehyde has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Improves metabolic syndrome symptoms in rodents and humans.

claims.claim: Mild blood cholesterol-lowering effect in animals and humans
claims.mechanism: Not fully specified; likely related to hydroxycinnamaldehyde and other extracts

consumption.amount: Regular use in cooking and teas
consumption.preparation: Ground cinnamon loses fragrance quickly. Buy fresh or grind sticks (shelf life up to 3 years). Add to teas, poached fruit, lentil soup.
consumption.reasoning: Multiple metabolic benefits from regular consumption

claims.claim: Speeds up gastric emptying, promotes satiety and increases thermic effect of food, helping prevent obesity
claims.mechanism: Active molecule gingerol (converted to zingerone when cooked). Studies in healthy men and women showed these effects.

claims.claim: May reduce nausea and vomiting during chemotherapy
claims.mechanism: Gingerol's effect on digestive tract motility and nausea receptors

consumption.amount: Thumb-sized piece fresh, or dried powder
consumption.frequency: daily or several times per week
consumption.preparation: Finely chopped in hot dishes, thinly sliced and pickled, or raw in smoothies. Use with caution before surgery (blood-thinning effect, ref 101).
consumption.reasoning: Versatile flavour profile, complements both savoury and sweet dishes

relationships.description: Both are rhizomes. Ginger's COX-2 pathway complements turmeric's NF-kB pathway for broader anti-inflammatory coverage.

claims.claim: Contains high levels of apigenin which inhibits proliferation of tumour cells in cell culture
claims.mechanism: Flavone apigenin directly inhibits tumour cell proliferation

consumption.preparation: Finely chopped leaves, used in tomato sauce, salsa verde, tabouli, falafel, and as garnish
consumption.reasoning: Fresh peppery grassy flavour complements most ingredients

claims.claim: Contains rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid with potential antioxidant, CNS-protective and liver-protective properties
claims.mechanism: Rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid are potent antioxidant compounds with neuroprotective and hepatoprotective activity

consumption.amount: Regular use in cooking and as herbal tea
consumption.preparation: Fresh or dried. Good as herbal tea. Grows well in pots near windows.
consumption.reasoning: Member of Lamiaceae family with high hydroxycinnamic acid content

claims.claim: One of the richest sources of potent antioxidants with potential cognitive-enhancing and protective effects
claims.mechanism: Contains an impressive range of biologically active compounds. Medieval School of Medicine considered it essential for health.
claims.study_ref: Ref 108 in book (animal studies + preliminary human trials)

consumption.amount: Regular use as herbal tea and cooking herb
consumption.preparation: As tea or cooking herb. Grows well at home in pots.
consumption.reasoning: Extremely high polyphenol content: 12073mg per 100g dried

claims.claim: Eating whole grains has protective effect against obesity, type 2 diabetes, cardiovascular disease and cancer
claims.mechanism: Bran contains phyto-protectants (ferulic acid, alkylresorcinols, apigenin, lignans, phytic acid) with antioxidative and anticarcinogenic activities. Germ contains spermidine which extends lifespan in animal models by removing damaged cells and reducing inflammation.
claims.study_ref: Refs 113-127 in book (multiple large epidemiological studies)

claims.claim: Highest whole grain intake associated with 17% lower all-cause mortality (NIH-AARP study)
claims.mechanism: Multiple pathways: fibre, phytochemicals, minerals, spermidine
claims.study_ref: Ref 121 in book (NIH-AARP Diet and Health Study)

claims.claim: Two servings per day associated with 21% reduction in type 2 diabetes risk
claims.mechanism: Beta-glucan fibre lowers glucose, insulin and cholesterol by reducing intestinal absorption

claims.claim: Three to five servings daily associated with 21% lower cardiovascular disease risk
claims.mechanism: Multiple pathways including cholesterol reduction and anti-inflammatory effects

consumption.preparation: Always buy unrefined. Combine with legumes for complete amino acid profile. Store flours in fridge to prevent rancidity. Germinating grains increases bioavailability of folate, tocopherols and tocotrienols.
consumption.reasoning: Refining removes 80% of thiamine, B vitamins, minerals, spermidine and essential fatty acids

relationships.description: Grains are low in lysine and threonine; legumes complement these missing amino acids for complete protein

claims.claim: Beta-glucan fibre in barley lowers glucose, insulin and cholesterol levels
claims.mechanism: Beta-glucan (found in appreciable amounts only in barley and oats) reduces intestinal absorption of cholesterol and glucose

consumption.preparation: Use hulled barley (not pearled, which has bran and germ removed). Toast before cooking for nutty flavour. Use in soups, breads, risotto, paella, pilaf. Can be made into barley tea or coffee-like drink.
consumption.reasoning: Hulled barley retains all nutrients. Rich in fibre, riboflavin, thiamine, iron, copper, zinc. Some cultivars have 10x more chromium than brewer's yeast.

relationships.description: Both uniquely contain beta-glucan fibre for cholesterol and glucose management

claims.claim: Consuming legumes and unrefined grains reduces glucose levels at the meal consumed AND at subsequent meals
claims.mechanism: Second-meal effect: eating quinoa and chickpeas at dinner reduces night-long blood glucose. Effect lost if grains are highly milled or legumes overcooked at high temperatures.

claims.claim: Combined with whole grains provides all essential amino acids without atherogenic fatty acids
claims.mechanism: Complementary amino acid profiles between legumes and grains. No saturated or trans-fatty acids unlike animal products.

consumption.preparation: Simmer dried beans, lentils or chickpeas 1-2 hours. Cook ahead and store in fridge. Use as side dish, in soups, or as salad base with fresh vegetables.
consumption.reasoning: Staple food of gladiators in Ancient Rome and of populations with healthy centenarians

relationships.description: Together provide complete essential amino acids without atherogenic fats

claims.claim: Consumption associated with lower risk of developing cancer and cardiovascular disease
claims.mechanism: Second highest protein content of legumes (27%). One of highest concentrations of phenolic compounds and antioxidants among legumes.

consumption.preparation: Cook 15-20 min (red) or 10 min in pressure cooker. Sprout for salads. Use in soups, dahl, pasta sauces, masala dosa.
consumption.reasoning: 1 cup boiled green lentils: 230 cal, 16g fibre, 82% RDA iron, 90% RDA folate

claims.claim: Concentrated source of protein, fibre, vitamins, minerals and low-glycaemic starches
claims.mechanism: Complex carbohydrates with high amylose content slowly digested, low glycaemic index

consumption.preparation: Soak 24 hours, cook 1 hour. Use in salads, soups, hummus, falafel, farinata.
consumption.reasoning: 1 cup boiled: 270 cal, 12g fibre, 15g protein, 64% RDA copper, 73% RDA manganese, 40% RDA folate

relationships.description: Combined in hummus for complete amino acid profile and healthy fats

claims.claim: Soy consumption modestly lowers blood pressure and improves arterial health. Isoflavones reduce osteoporosis, cancer risk and hot flush severity.
claims.mechanism: Soy isoflavones (genistein, daidzein) mimic oestrogen, binding to oestrogen receptors and blocking cancer cell proliferation. Soy protein replaces animal fats to reduce LDL cholesterol.

consumption.amount: Regular servings of tofu, edamame, miso, tempeh
consumption.preparation: Tofu: grill thin slices or add cubes to soups last 10-15 min. Edamame: boil pods in salted water. Miso: dissolve in soup.
consumption.reasoning: Soy protein quality similar to animal protein. 100g tofu: 10g protein, 91 cal. 1 cup edamame: 18g protein, 121% RDA folate.

relationships.description: Soybeans (genistein) + quercetin-rich foods (capers, apples) block prostate and ovarian cancer cell growth at lower doses than either alone

claims.claim: Six-month RCT showed eating cholesterol-lowering foods including almonds led to 15% reduction in blood cholesterol
claims.mechanism: Rich in vitamin E (antioxidant), monounsaturated fats, soluble fibre that reduces intestinal cholesterol absorption

consumption.preparation: Raw preferred. Soak overnight to reduce phytic acid and improve digestion. Use chopped in salads or as almond milk.
consumption.reasoning: 30g provides 37% RDA vitamin E, 20% RDA magnesium

claims.claim: Excellent source of omega-3 alpha-linolenic acid with cardioprotective properties
claims.mechanism: 100g provides 9g alpha-linolenic acid, converted to DHA and EPA. These omega-3 reduce inflammation, platelet aggregation, triglycerides. Rich in vitamin E and polyphenols preventing LDL oxidation.

consumption.frequency: daily or several times per week
consumption.preparation: Raw, added to salads or smoothies
consumption.reasoning: Main plant source of cardioprotective omega-3 fatty acids

claims.claim: Richest food source of selenium, a powerful antioxidant mineral preventing free radical tissue damage
claims.mechanism: 6 nuts supply ~780% RDA selenium. Selenium is cofactor for glutathione peroxidase, a key antioxidant enzyme.

consumption.preparation: Raw, added to morning smoothies with dark cocoa powder
consumption.reasoning: Very high selenium concentration means small amounts suffice

claims.claim: Consuming whole flaxseed results in significant reduction in total and LDL-cholesterol
claims.mechanism: Richest plant source of antioxidant lignans (up to 13mg/g) and omega-3 alpha-linolenic acid. Takes <2 weeks for omega-3 to incorporate into cell membranes. Enterolignans (produced by gut bacteria from lignans) associated with lower heart disease and cancer risk.

consumption.preparation: GRIND before eating (improves omega-3 and enterolignan bioavailability). Keep refrigerated to prevent oxidation of polyunsaturated fats.
consumption.reasoning: After oily fish, the main dietary source of omega-3. Grinding is essential for absorption.

claims.claim: Sesamin (a lignan in sesame) associated with reduced serum cholesterol in rodents and humans
claims.mechanism: Sesamin modulates hepatic cholesterol metabolism

consumption.amount: Regular use as tahini or whole seeds
consumption.preparation: Whole in salads, ground as tahini for hummus and dips. Unhulled more nutrient-rich. Black seeds have toasty smoky flavour. Lightly toast for snacks.
consumption.reasoning: Called 'seeds of longevity', oldest known oilseed crop

claims.claim: 3-day-old broccoli sprouts contain 10-100x higher glucoraphanin levels than mature plants, highly effective in reducing breast cancer incidence in animals
claims.mechanism: Glucoraphanin is converted to sulforaphane, a potent activator of phase 2 detoxification enzymes via Nrf2 pathway

consumption.preparation: Sprout at home: soak 8-24 hours, spread on tray with damp towel, harvest when tiny sprouts appear. Consume within 3-4 days, keep refrigerated. Buy only seeds suitable for home sprouting.
consumption.reasoning: Extreme concentration of cancer-fighting compounds vs mature broccoli

relationships.description: Same sulforaphane pathway but 10-100x more concentrated

claims.claim: Wild blueberries and blackberries contain highest phenolic phytochemical content among fruits, contributing to lower inflammation
claims.mechanism: Anthocyanins and proanthocyanidins with potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activity

consumption.amount: Regular servings as dessert or in smoothies
consumption.frequency: daily or several times per week
consumption.preparation: Fresh or frozen. Low-glycaemic fruit rich in vitamins with skin-protecting properties.
consumption.reasoning: Highest polyphenol content: 836mg per 100g (lowbush) or 560mg (highbush)

claims.claim: Can potentially lower inflammation, blood pressure and artery plaque build-up. Small trials suggest may inhibit prostate tumour growth.
claims.mechanism: Very rich in polyphenols, especially punicalagin (responsible for ~50% of juice's antioxidant activity). May lower PSA levels.
claims.study_ref: Refs 210, 211, 212, 213, 214 in book. Not confirmed in larger trials (ref 215).

consumption.preparation: Fresh seeds or juice
consumption.reasoning: Extremely high polyphenol content with unique punicalagin

claims.claim: 4+ servings per week of omega-3 rich fish lowers heart attack risk by 22%. Even 1-4 servings per month has significant cardioprotective effect.
claims.mechanism: EPA and DHA omega-3 fatty acids block impulses causing irregular heart rhythm (antiarrhythmic), lower triglycerides, decrease blood pressure and inflammation, decrease platelet aggregation, improve vascular function.
claims.study_ref: Refs 216, 217, 218, 219, 223, 224, 225 in book (large observational studies + RCTs)

claims.claim: Broiled/baked fish 3+ times/week: 49% lower heart attack death risk and 58% lower sudden cardiac death risk. Fried fish provides NO protection and may increase risk.
claims.mechanism: Commercially fried seafood cooked in partially hydrogenated oils containing trans-fat negates benefits

claims.claim: Fish exceeds meat in vitamin B12 bioavailability and unlike red meat, consumption associated with lower colon cancer risk
claims.mechanism: High B12 bioavailability without the carcinogenic compounds in red meat

consumption.preparation: Broiled or baked, NEVER deep-fried. Cold-water species preferred.
consumption.reasoning: Fried fish in hydrogenated oils eliminates all cardiovascular benefit

claims.claim: Reduces plasma triglycerides, oxidative stress markers, and increases HDL-cholesterol. Phenolics inhibit inflammation and thrombosis.
claims.mechanism: Contains 30+ phytocompounds including oleuropein, tyrosol, hydroxytyrosol. 100g provides up to 25mg alpha-tocopherol, 20-500mg oleuropein, 98-185mg phytosterols.

claims.claim: Contains oleocanthal that prevents blood clots. 50g daily equivalent to a baby aspirin for heart disease prevention.
claims.mechanism: Oleocanthal has structural profile similar to ibuprofen. Anti-inflammatory and anti-platelet aggregation. The pungent, peppery throat sensation indicates oleocanthal presence.

consumption.amount: Use as primary condiment. 1 tablespoon = ~120 calories, balance with activity.
consumption.preparation: MUST be first cold-pressed extra-virgin. Look for harvest date, consume within 12-14 months. Use for dressing salads, vegetables, legumes, pasta. Do not overheat.
consumption.reasoning: Fresher oil = higher phytocompound concentration. 'Olive oil' (refined) lacks the bioactive compounds.

relationships.description: Fat enables absorption of fat-soluble carotenoids
Fat enables absorption of fat-soluble vitamins K, A, E
Fat-soluble curcuminoids require dietary fat for absorption

claims.claim: Both green and black tea prevent cancer in variety of animal models (skin, lung, mouth, oesophagus, stomach, colon, pancreas, bladder, prostate). Small RCT suggests green tea catechins prevent precancerous prostate lesions turning cancerous.
claims.mechanism: EGCG (epigallocatechin gallate, 60% of catechin content) promotes self-destruction of tumour cells while sparing healthy cells. Reverses endothelial dysfunction in smokers and coronary artery disease patients.
claims.study_ref: Refs 237-246 in book (animal models, small clinical trial)

consumption.preparation: Brew with hot water. Alternate with sage or rosemary infusions.
consumption.reasoning: Powerful antioxidant and anti-cancer phytochemicals in every cup

claims.claim: 3-4 cups per day achieves largest risk reduction for cardiovascular and cancer outcomes
claims.mechanism: Not fully specified in this chapter; epidemiological association

consumption.preparation: Filter coffee
consumption.reasoning: Optimal dose from epidemiological data

claims.claim: Plain dark chocolate (rich in epicatechin) increases plasma antioxidant capacity. Effect markedly reduced when consumed with milk.
claims.mechanism: Epicatechin is a potent antioxidant flavonoid. Milk proteins bind to epicatechin preventing absorption.

consumption.preparation: Plain dark chocolate only, NOT milk chocolate. Consume without dairy.
consumption.reasoning: Milk blocks the antioxidant absorption (Nature study)

relationships.description: Author combines dark cocoa powder with brazil nuts in morning smoothies

claims.claim: Relatively high concentration of vegetable omega-3 fatty acids
claims.mechanism: 50g fresh purslane contains approximately 200mg alpha-linolenic acid

consumption.amount: Add to salads from time to time
consumption.preparation: Raw stems and leaves in salads. Slightly sour and salty taste.
consumption.reasoning: Wild edible weed with unusually high omega-3 for a plant
